dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6633 IH: High-Capacity Grid Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.official-title: To establish a best-available transmission conductor standard for certain transmission projects under the Federal Power Act and to provide for presumptions regarding cost recovery for such conductors.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the High-Capacity Grid Act.
Section 205 of the Federal Power Act (16 U.S.C. 824d) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: The term best-available transmission conductor means a transmission conductor that, as determined by the Commission by rule under paragraph (5)—

clause.text: provides the greatest feasible and commercially available energy-carrying capacity at a given voltage level;
provides the highest feasible and commercially available electrical efficiency at that voltage level; and
mitigates thermal sag at the maximum rated transmission-carrying capacity of the facility.
the construction of a new transmission facility subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission under section 201(b); and
any modification, upgrade, replacement, or reconductoring of an existing transmission line subject to such jurisdiction.

paragraph.header: Presumption of prudence for best-available transmission conductors
paragraph.text: In any filing seeking to recover the cost of a transmission conductor for a covered project, and in any proceeding to determine whether such cost may be recovered through rates, the Commission shall presume that the use of a best-available transmission conductor is a prudent practice and that the associated costs are just and reasonable.

paragraph.header: Presumption against recovery of costs for non-best-available conductors
paragraph.text: In any filing seeking to recover the cost of a transmission conductor for a covered project, and in any proceeding to determine whether such cost may be recovered through rates, the Commission shall presume that the use of a transmission conductor that is not a best-available transmission conductor is not prudent and that the associated costs are not just and reasonable.
The requirements of this subsection apply only to public utilities and only with respect to covered projects that are subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission under section 201(b).

Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Commission shall promulgate regulations to implement this subsection. In promulgating such regulations, the Commission shall—

subparagraph.text: establish a methodology for determining whether a transmission conductor is a best-available transmission conductor for purposes of this subsection;
ensure that such methodology is consistent with the criteria set forth in paragraph (1)(A); and
provide for periodic review and updating of the methodology to reflect improvements in technology, materials, and system performance.
